display-hero.purpose: Earned brand moments only — onboarding, the dashboard greeting deck. The ceiling of the app scale (40px).

body.purpose: Standard body and prose; cap measured text at 65-75ch (14px).

body-sm.purpose: Dense UI text, control labels, buttons (13px).

label.purpose: Mono uppercase eyebrows, nav labels, status pills — the one 500-weight in the system (Fira Code 12px, +0.6px tracking).

mono-num.purpose: Counts, IDs, diffs, timestamps — tabular figures (Fira Code 13px).

shadows.name: soft
shadows.purpose: Subtle warm lift for card hover and sticky chrome that must read as slightly raised.

shadows.name: golden-float
shadows.purpose: Signature multi-layer golden-hour cascade. Genuinely floating data surfaces only: dialogs, popovers, drawers, toasts, hero deck. Amber-tinted, offset lower-left.

shadows.name: focus-ring
shadows.purpose: Accent focus glow, paired with a 2px solid #fa520f outline at 2px offset. On every interactive element.

motion.name: slow
motion.purpose: Sidebar collapse, drawer, larger chrome (emphasized = ease-out-cubic).

motion.name: reduced
motion.purpose: CcMotion.resolve collapses every duration to zero under prefers-reduced-motion / MediaQuery.disableAnimations.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The main CTA. Ink-black at rest, warms to Signal Orange on hover — calm by default, orange on intent.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { font-family: 'Manrope', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 1.45; font-weight: 400; color: #ffffff; background: #1f1f1f; border: none; border-radius: 2px; padding: 12px 18px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 120ms ease-out, transform 120ms ease-out; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #fa520f; } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: translateY(1px); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #fa520f; outline-offset: 2px; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(250,82,15,0.12); }

components.name: Accent Button
components.description: Solid Signal Orange 'go' affordance for in-app actions where a dark button would feel too heavy. Spend within the two-per-screen accent budget.
components.css: .ds-btn-accent { font-family: 'Manrope', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 1.45; font-weight: 400; color: #ffffff; background: #fa520f; border: none; border-radius: 2px; padding: 9px 16px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 120ms ease-out, transform 120ms ease-out; } .ds-btn-accent:hover { background: #fb6424; } .ds-btn-accent:active { background: #dc480d; transform: translateY(1px); } .ds-btn-accent:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #fa520f; outline-offset: 2px; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(250,82,15,0.12); }

components.name: Line Button
components.description: Quiet utility button for dense UI: white fill, hairline border that strengthens to ink on hover.
components.css: .ds-btn-line { font-family: 'Manrope', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 1.45; font-weight: 400; color: #1f1f1f; background: #ffffff; border: 1px solid #e8e5dc; border-radius: 2px; padding: 9px 16px; cursor: pointer; transition: border-color 120ms ease-out, transform 120ms ease-out; } .ds-btn-line:hover { border-color: #1f1f1f; } .ds-btn-line:active { transform: translateY(1px); } .ds-btn-line:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #fa520f; outline-offset: 2px; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(250,82,15,0.12); }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: White, hairline, 2px corners. Focus swaps the border to accent and adds the focus ring; placeholder uses the lightest gray, real hints stay in muted.
components.css: .ds-input { font-family: 'Manrope', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 1.45; color: #1f1f1f; background: #ffffff; border: 1px solid #e8e5dc; border-radius: 2px; padding: 9px 12px; transition: border-color 120ms ease-out, box-shadow 120ms ease-out; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #8c8578; } .ds-input:focus { outline: none; border-color: #fa520f; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(250,82,15,0.12); }

components.name: Status Pill — Running
components.description: Live presence pill. Color + animated equalizer bars + uppercase label, so state survives color-blind viewing and grayscale. Bars settle under reduced motion.
components.css: .ds-status-run { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; font-family: 'Fira Code', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.6px; text-transform: uppercase; color: #17a34a; background: rgba(23,163,74,0.14); border-radius: 999px; padding: 3px 8px; } .ds-status-run .ds-eq { display: inline-flex; align-items: flex-end; gap: 2px; height: 10px; } .ds-status-run .ds-eq i { width: 2px; background: #17a34a; border-radius: 1px; animation: ds-eq 900ms ease-in-out infinite; } .ds-status-run .ds-eq i:nth-child(1) { height: 5px; animation-delay: 0ms; } .ds-status-run .ds-eq i:nth-child(2) { height: 10px; animation-delay: 150ms; } .ds-status-run .ds-eq i:nth-child(3) { height: 7px; animation-delay: 300ms; } @keyframes ds-eq { 0%, 100% { transform: scaleY(0.5); } 50% { transform: scaleY(1); } } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .ds-status-run .ds-eq i { animation: none; transform: scaleY(0.7); } }

components.name: Status Pill — Failed
components.description: Danger-tinted pill with an alert glyph and label. Color is never the only signal.
components.html: Failed
components.css: .ds-status-failed { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 5px; font-family: 'Fira Code', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.6px; text-transform: uppercase; color: #dc2626; background: rgba(220,38,38,0.12); border-radius: 999px; padding: 3px 8px; }

components.name: Data Card
components.description: The tight 2px list/rail unit. Status-relevant cards tint the BORDER (not the fill) by state, so a busy fleet stays legible. Never nest a card in a card.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #ffffff; border: 1px solid #e8e5dc; border-radius: 2px; padding: 11px 12px; transition: box-shadow 120ms ease-out; } .ds-card:hover { box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(127,99,21,0.05), -2px 6px 18px rgba(127,99,21,0.05); } .ds-card-title { font-family: 'Manrope', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; line-height: 1.5; color: #1f1f1f; } .ds-card-meta { margin-top: 2px; font-family: 'Fira Code', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 12px; color: #3d3d3d; }

components.name: Keyboard Key
components.description: Mono key cap for the keyboard-first surface. Pairs with shortcut hints like ⌘K.
components.css: .ds-kbd { font-family: 'Fira Code', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 12px; color: #3d3d3d; background: #fcfbf9; border: 1px solid #e8e5dc; border-radius: 2px; padding: 1px 5px; }

narrative.overview: Control Center is the operator deck for a one-person developer operations hub: a single technical operator running many concurrent streams of work — coding agents on isolated worktrees, a meeting recording, a calendar filling up, PRs to review, feeds and conversations updating — and holding all of it in view at once. The personality is alive, warm, confident: alive because the surface reports real work as it happens, warm in the Anthropic register (intelligent, on-your-side, never cold or corporate), confident because it is direct and technical with no hype. The canvas is near-white with ink-black as the structural color; the warm gold is confined to bounded graphics (the logo mosaic, one golden-hour horizon, the dark sunset CTA) and the single orange signal is rationed to at most twice per screen. Warmth is a moment you earn at a threshold, not a wash you apply everywhere — which is how the system reads warm and confident while staying quiet and dense for ten-hours-a-day use across desktop, web, and a touch-first phone remote.
keyCharacteristics: Near-white canvas, ink-black structure, one rationed orange signal — black does the structural work, orange is a signal not a highlight.
Earned warmth — golden-hour gold lives only in bounded brand graphics and the amber elevation shadows, never as a page wash.
Sharp 2px geometry — near-zero radius everywhere (4px on large panels, pills excepted).
One family, near-one weight — Manrope + Fira Code, weight 400 throughout except the mono eyebrow label at 500; hierarchy is size + color, never bold.
Presence over decoration — motion and color report real state (running / blocked / failed / recording / syncing) or they are cut; every animated element has a reduced-motion path.
Status is never color alone — every state pairs color with an icon/shape and a text label.
Dual theme — a full light and dark theme ship from the same token names.
Multi-platform, one operation — dense + keyboard-first on desktop, ≥44px touch targets on the phone remote, no surface a degraded afterthought.

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: Signal Orange appears on ≤2 elements per screen. Its rarity is the signal; spend it on the one thing that matters next, never on decoration.

rules.name: The Earned-Warmth Rule
rules.body: All warm gold is confined to bounded graphics (mosaic, horizon, sunset CTA) and the amber elevation shadows. Gold is never a text color and never a page background.

rules.name: The Contrast Rule
rules.body: Body and essential text target 7:1 (AAA); fg (~14:1) and muted (~9:1) on the canvas clear it and are the defaults. AA (4.5:1) is the minimum, never the target. The lightest grays (placeholder ~3.3:1, idle) clear only the disabled-state bar; never carry meaningful text in them.

rules.name: The Derivation Contract
rules.body: Alpha tokens (idle, line-strong, accent-soft, the *-soft set, hovers) are an opacity of a base token — idle = fg @ 38%, accent-soft = accent @ 12%. Keep the relationship in code so a base-color change propagates.

rules.name: The One-Weight Rule
rules.body: Everything is Manrope 400 — even Display Hero at 40px. Hierarchy comes from size and color, never bold. The sole 500 is the mono label. Never introduce a 700, a second display family, or Inter/Roboto/Arial as a display face.

rules.name: The Machine-Truth Rule
rules.body: Anything countable, addressable, or time-stamped is Fira Code with tabular figures. Mono signals machine truth, not decoration.

rules.name: The Warm-Shadow Rule
rules.body: Shadows are always amber-tinted (rgba(127,99,21,…), never cool gray) and offset to the lower-left, as if lit by late-afternoon sun. Never a cool, symmetric, or top-down drop shadow.

rules.name: The Borders-Not-Shadows Rule
rules.body: A surface separates with a hairline ring by default. Reach for a shadow only when something genuinely floats above the canvas.

rules.name: The Always-Visible-Focus Rule
rules.body: Every interactive element shows a focus ring: a 2px solid accent outline at 2px offset plus a 0 0 0 3px accent-soft glow. Keyboard operability is a P0; never remove the ring without an equal replacement.

dos: Do keep the canvas near-white and let ink-black be the structural color; reserve pure white for data surfaces.
Do confine all warm gold to bounded graphics — mosaic, horizon, sunset CTA — and amber elevation shadows.
Do ration the accent to two uses per screen; default primary buttons to dark and let orange arrive on hover.
Do pair every status with an icon/shape and a text label, never color alone.
Do target AAA contrast (7:1) for body and essential text; keep muted as the lightest color you trust for meaningful text, AA (4.5:1) as the hard floor.
Do keep one family at weight 400 (the mono label 500 is the only exception); build hierarchy from size and color.
Do hold corners at 2px (4px for large panels; pills excepted).
Do make warm, lower-left, amber-tinted shadows — and only on genuinely floating surfaces.
Do set numerics, IDs, counts, and timestamps in mono with tabular figures.
Do ship a real reduced-motion path for every animated/presence element, and keep the 2px accent focus ring visible on every interactive element.
Do design every surface for its platform: dense + keyboard-first on desktop, ≥44px touch targets and no hover-only affordances on the phone remote, and verify both light and dark themes.
donts: Don't let any surface read as a generic SaaS dashboard — no gradient hero-metric cards, no identical rounded card grids repeated down a page, no decorative charts, no purple gradients.
Don't ship the default component-kit / template look — distinction comes from making the model (agents, meetings, conversations) legible, not from decoration.
Don't use ivory/cream as a full-bleed surface — that's the 'ugly yellow' the near-white canvas exists to fix.
Don't use pure #000, cool grays, or cool/symmetric drop shadows.
Don't introduce blue/green/purple chrome or any cool gradient (status hues excepted, and always shape-paired).
Don't add a bold weight or a second display typeface.
Don't carry meaningful text in placeholder or idle grays — they clear the disabled bar, not the 4.5:1 body bar.
Don't round corners to feel friendlier, or add radius to hero imagery; sharpness is the point.
Don't spend the accent on decoration — it is a signal, not a highlight; and never lead the block mosaic with yellow.
Don't nest a card inside a card, and don't let color be the only carrier of meaning (status, validation, diffs).
